human.summary: Pre-registered prediction: applying ETCC + constraint geometry to seawater chemistry yields a stable resonance at 96.79 THz (≈ 3.097 µm wavelength). This DTU is the date-stamped pre-registration so a later measurement either confirms or falsifies the framework's first-order claim.
bullets: Substrate: seawater (variable salinity assumed; nominal 35 PSU)
Falsification rule: if measurement at the spec apparatus finds no detectable resonance peak within ±0.2 THz of 96.79 THz under the documented conditions, the framework's predictive claim is rejected.
Confirmation rule: detectable peak at 96.79 ± 0.2 THz that does not appear in any pre-registered competing model.
Pre-registration date: 2026-05-07. No measurement was taken before this DTU was minted.

definitions.definition: STSVK-002 framework: stable substrates occupy a narrow constraint cone in transduction phase space. Applying the cone equations to a known-substrate chemistry yields a predicted resonance frequency at the cone's stability minimum.
Frequency at which energy transduction through the substrate has minimum dissipation; corresponds to a fixed point of the constraint-driven dynamics.

invariants: Pre-registration timestamp must precede any measurement attempt for the test to qualify as a real prediction.
Falsification threshold: ±0.2 THz around 96.79 THz; outside this band counts as a miss.
Confirmation must NOT be a frequency already predicted by an established competing model (water O-H stretch ≈ 102 THz is the nearest known mode and is excluded as confounder).
claims: If the framework is first-order (i.e. derives observations rather than fitting them), this prediction was generated PRIOR to any measurement and survives independent verification.
If the prediction fails, the framework's claim to first-order status is rejected — it joins the larger class of 'internally consistent design ontologies that don't predict reality.'
If it succeeds at 96.79 ± 0.2 THz, the next test is to derive 2-3 more falsifiable predictions in unrelated domains and have them survive independent measurement.
examples: Apparatus spec (planned, not yet built): tunable mid-IR laser sweep across 95-99 THz against a 35-PSU seawater sample at 25°C, transmission spectroscopy. Looking for absorption / transmission anomaly at the predicted frequency.

Competing model exclusion: H2O has known IR modes at 3,400 cm^-1 (≈102 THz O-H stretch), 1,650 cm^-1 (≈49.5 THz bend), and a librational band centered ~640 cm^-1 (≈19 THz). 96.79 THz lies in a region without a known dominant mode for pure water; the seawater hypothesis specifically predicts a mode emerges from ion-water constraint geometry that pure water lacks.
nextActions: Run measurement WITHOUT consulting this DTU first (independent operator strongly preferred).
Mint a result DTU citing this one as parent. Either outcome — confirmation or falsification — should be publicly recorded with raw data.

math.interpretation: Spectral signature of an ETCC stability point in seawater transduction

steps: Verify pre-registration timestamp predates measurement timestamp.
If < 0.2 AND independent operator AND no competing model predicted this exact band → confirmed.
If > 0.2 OR no peak detected → falsified.
If competing model already predicted within band → ambiguous, run secondary test.
